data.text: I have a disk image and a text note; I’ll analyze the disk image first and also extract IOCs from both artifacts in parallel to surface anything actionable quickly.
The disk-image parser found the partition table and embedded strings/IOCs; I’ll record that, then enrich the IP and domains via public lookups to assess whether they merit network pivots.

args.rationale: The case asks for partition parsing, filesystem identification, printable strings, and any embedded indicators or credentials in the carved image.
args.expected: I expected either a malformed small partition table with little content or residual strings in slack space that could reveal infrastructure or credentials.
args.found: analyze_disk_image on artifact 9a508ece-4ed2-4271-a674-d5391a1d42f6 (execution_log_id e8921ca9-b97b-42b0-b4ff-5c616604603c) reported a 4096-byte raw_dd image with partitionScheme 'mbr', 1 partition entry: index 0, type 'linux', bootable true, startLba 1, sizeLba 7. No filesystem signatures were detected (detectedFilesystems: []). The same result surfaced notable strings including 'exfil endpoint: https://exfil-c2.evil-domain.org/dump', 'callback URL: http://malware-staging.xyz/beacon.php', 'scheduled task: cron @reboot /opt/.k/run.sh', 'last beacon: 2026-05-22T18:44:12Z OK', 'implant pass: P@ssw0rd-from-disk', 'C2 server: 91.219.236.142', and 'implant user: deploy'. A parallel extract_iocs run on the same artifact (execution_log_id 5bd47c99-362f-47ca-b2bd-e90ab2c94b9f) returned totalCount 0, which does not negate the disk-image parser's embeddedIndicators output and should be treated as a tooling discrepancy.
args.next_step: Enrich the embedded IP and domains/URLs with public lookups and assess whether the recovered strings support a likely malicious staging/exfil hypothesis.

No filesystem signatures were detected (detectedFilesystems: []). The same result surfaced notable strings including 'exfil endpoint: https://exfil-c2.evil-domain.org/dump', 'callback URL: http://malware-staging.xyz/beacon.php', 'scheduled task: cron @reboot /opt/.k/run.sh', 'last beacon: 2026-05-22T18:44:12Z OK', 'implant pass: P@ssw0rd-from-disk', 'C2 server: 91.219.236.142', and 'implant user: deploy'. A parallel extract_iocs run on the same artifact (execution_log_id 5bd47c99-362f-47ca-b2bd-e90ab2c94b9f) returned totalCount 0, which does not negate the disk-image parser's embeddedIndicators output and should be treated as a tooling discrepancy.

data.text: I’ll enrich the one IP and two domains in parallel.
The enrichments are sparse but enough to place the IP on public hosting; I’ll record the external context and the tooling discrepancy before concluding.

args.rationale: Public enrichment can distinguish an internal artifact from infrastructure that warrants pivoting into network telemetry.
args.expected: If the recovered indicators were operational, I expected at least geolocation/hosting details for the IP and possibly threat-intel reputation for the domains.
args.found: fetch_url for https://ipinfo.io/91.219.236.142/json (execution_log_id 95ee97dc-4fa2-4005-a58b-cda36f8ae4b7) returned status 200 with JSON identifying IP 91.219.236.142 as hostname '45601199-stellar.serverastra.com', city 'Budapest', country 'HU', org 'AS56322 ServerAstra Kft.'. OTX lookups for domains exfil-c2.evil-domain.org and malware-staging.xyz (execution_log_ids 056c2fbd-b2b4-4c24-9fe7-4a8eccd5fbf7 and e6556ded-ddbc-4140-b212-5c5dc78cf4d0) both returned status 200 but pulse_info.count 0, providing no positive reputation hits. The absence of OTX hits does not invalidate the disk-image strings; it only means public OTX had no pulses for those domains at lookup time.
args.next_step: Record the discrepancy between disk-image IOC extraction methods, then finalize with recommendations to pivot on the recovered IP, domains, URLs, and credential strings.

OTX lookups for domains exfil-c2.evil-domain.org and malware-staging.xyz (execution_log_ids 056c2fbd-b2b4-4c24-9fe7-4a8eccd5fbf7 and e6556ded-ddbc-4140-b212-5c5dc78cf4d0) both returned status 200 but pulse_info.count 0, providing no positive reputation hits. The absence of OTX hits does not invalidate the disk-image strings; it only means public OTX had no pulses for those domains at lookup time.

args.rationale: Two analysis methods were run against the same disk image and produced different IOC extraction outcomes; this needs to be documented explicitly.
args.expected: I expected extract_iocs to surface the same IPs/domains/URLs that analyze_disk_image found in embedded strings.
args.found: For artifact 9a508ece-4ed2-4271-a674-d5391a1d42f6, analyze_disk_image (execution_log_id e8921ca9-b97b-42b0-b4ff-5c616604603c) reported embeddedIndicators containing IPv4 91.219.236.142, domains exfil-c2.evil-domain.org and malware-staging.xyz, and URLs http://malware-staging.xyz/beacon.php and https://exfil-c2.evil-domain.org/dump. However, extract_iocs on the same artifact (execution_log_id 5bd47c99-362f-47ca-b2bd-e90ab2c94b9f) returned totalCount 0. I therefore rely on the disk-image parser for the recovered strings/indicators and treat the standalone IOC extractor result as a tool limitation or parsing mismatch for this artifact type.

data.text: I have enough to conclude: small unmountable carved MBR image, no filesystem signature, but multiple embedded strings consistent with staging/C2 and a credential.

args.summary: A 4096-byte carved raw disk image from WIN-FIN-07 contains an intact MBR with a single bootable Linux-type partition entry, but no detectable filesystem signatures. Despite the image being too small to mount meaningfully, disk-image analysis recovered embedded printable strings that include two URLs, two domains, one public IP, a username, a password string, a scheduled-task style persistence command, and a 'last beacon' timestamp. These strings are consistent with malicious staging or C2/exfiltration configuration preserved in unallocated slack rather than a normal recoverable filesystem. Public enrichment placed the recovered IP on a hosted server in Budapest, Hungary, while public OTX lookups for the domains returned no pulses.

iocs.context: Recovered from analyze_disk_image notable string 'C2 server: 91.219.236.142'; ipinfo identified hosted infrastructure at AS56322 ServerAstra Kft.
Recovered from embeddedIndicators and notable string 'exfil endpoint: https://exfil-c2.evil-domain.org/dump'.
Recovered from embeddedIndicators and notable string 'callback URL: http://malware-staging.xyz/beacon.php'.
Recovered from analyze_disk_image notable strings as an exfil endpoint.
Recovered from analyze_disk_image notable strings as a callback URL.
Recovered from notable string 'implant user: deploy'.
Recovered from notable string 'implant pass: P@ssw0rd-from-disk'.
Recovered from notable string 'scheduled task: cron @reboot /opt/.k/run.sh'.

ttps.name: Scheduled Task/Job
ttps.evidence: analyze_disk_image recovered the string 'scheduled task: cron @reboot /opt/.k/run.sh'.

ttps.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
ttps.evidence: Recovered URLs use HTTP and HTTPS: http://malware-staging.xyz/beacon.php and https://exfil-c2.evil-domain.org/dump.

ttps.name: Valid Accounts
ttps.evidence: analyze_disk_image recovered credential-like strings 'implant user: deploy' and 'implant pass: P@ssw0rd-from-disk'.

ttps.name: Ingress Tool Transfer / External Tooling
ttps.evidence: Recovered strings describe a 'callback URL' and 'exfil endpoint', consistent with remote staging or transfer over web protocols.

timeline.event: Recovered embedded string indicates 'last beacon: 2026-05-22T18:44:12Z OK'.

recommendations: Pivot in proxy, DNS, firewall, EDR, and netflow telemetry for 91.219.236.142, exfil-c2.evil-domain.org, malware-staging.xyz, http://malware-staging.xyz/beacon.php, and https://exfil-c2.evil-domain.org/dump.
Search WIN-FIN-07 and adjacent hosts for the path /opt/.k/run.sh and any cron or autorun artifacts matching '@reboot'.
Treat the recovered 'deploy' / 'P@ssw0rd-from-disk' strings as potentially valid credentials until disproven: check for account existence, reset if present, and review authentication logs for misuse.
Examine endpoint telemetry on WIN-FIN-07 around and after 2026-05-22T18:44:12Z for outbound web sessions, process launches, script execution, or file access consistent with beaconing or exfiltration.

Because the image shows a Linux-type partition entry on a Windows finance laptop, verify whether the artifact reflects removable media, a VM image, WSL/container remnants, or attacker tooling staged in raw sectors.
Note the analysis discrepancy: standalone extract_iocs returned no indicators on the same artifact, so preserve the original image and, if needed, re-validate with additional carving/string tools.
